LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAROLINE MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MILLER PRODUCES PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT SHE DID NOT OWN OR CONTROL THE TAXI CAB THAT WAS INVOLVED IN THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 3.) MILLER ALSO PRODUCES PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT SHE WAS NOT IN A JOINT ENTERPRISE OR PARTNERSHIP WITH DEFENDANT SANCHEZ AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT AS TO RENDER HER VICARIOUSLY LIABLE FOR DEFENDANT SANCHEZ'S ACTS. (UMF 4-8.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT IS DIRECTLY OR VICARIOUSLY LIABILITY. EVIDENCE THAT MILLER OWNED A MEDALLION, AND A SHARE OF CORPORATE DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT IS NOT DISPOSITIVE THAT DEFENDANT WAS IN A JOINT VENTURE OR PARTNERSHIP WITH DEFENDANT SANCHEZ. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENTS REGARDING ALTER EGO AND VEHICLE CODE 17150 WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED AS THE ISSUES ARE NOT IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

REPLY DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING'S REPLY TO PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS' SEPARATE STATEMENT AND RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL MATERIAL FACTS FILED BY DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC (FROM NON-LEAD CASE# 519794)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT IS TREATING THIS AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. DEFENDANT MET ITS BURDEN IN ESTABLISHING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED A DUTY OF CARE UNDER THE TWO THEORIES OF LIABILITY PLED IN THE COMPLAINT - DIRECT OR VICARIOUS LIABILITY. THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTED TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF DID NOT ESTABLISH THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT UNDER EITHER THEORY PLED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHED THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE OWNERSHIP OF THE VEHICLE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADEQUATELY PLED THE ELEMENTS OF VEH. CODE SEC. 17150 IN THE COMPLAINT. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 45 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT, IF SHE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF VEH. CODE SEC. 17150. "IF SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED ON THE GROUND THAT THE COMPLAINT IS LEGALLY INSUFFICIENT, BUT IT APPEARS FROM THE MATERIALS SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION THAT THE PLAINTIFF COULD STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION, THE TRIAL COURT SHOULD GIVE THE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT BEFORE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT." (BOSTROM V. COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO (1995) 35 CAL.APP.4TH 1654, 1663.) HERE, SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THAT DEFENDANT GAVE THE DRIVER PERMISSION TO USE THE VEHICLE, A REQUISITE ELEMENT OF VEH. CODE SEC. 17150. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. In its notice of motion, Defendant first seeks to strike the language in paragraph 29 of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") as follows: "Defendants Taxi Equipment Leasing'recklessly and negligently owned, operated, and/or controlled the Nissan Taxi Cab driven by Sanchez." However, paragraph 29 of the FAC states "Plaintiff incorporates herein by reference, as though set forth in full, Paragraphs 1 through 28 of this Complaint." Therefore, the motion as to this language is denied as improperly noticed. As to the alter ego allegations, the motion is denied. "California courts have a policy of great liberality in allowing amendments at any stage of the proceeding." (Douglas v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 155, 158.) It serves judicial economy to allow the amendment to stand, rather than require Plaintiff to file a motion for leave to amend, which would likely be granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The statute of limitations does not bar the third cause of action because it relates back to the original complaint. The relation back doctrine applies where (1) the new cause of action is based on the "same general set of facts" as the original; (2) seeks recovery against the same defendants for the "same injuries" and (3) refers to the "same incident" - i.e., the "same accident" caused by the "same offending instrumentality." (Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 150; Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) 6:735.) Here, the facts in the original complaint and the First Amended Complaint are identical, both complaints seek recovery against the same defendants for the same injuries, and both complaints refer to the same accident caused by the same offending instrumentality. Further, "in applying the relation-back analysis, courts should consider the strong policy in this state that cases should be decided on their merits." (Pointe San Diego Residential Community, L.P. v. Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves & Savitch, LLP (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 265, 277.) The Court did not consider the Declaration of Mark D. Rosenberg in ruling on the demurrer. A demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling WITH MODIFICATION: DEFENDANT TAXI EQUIPMENT LEASING LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. THE COURT IS TREATING THIS AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Defendant met its burden in establishing that Plaintiff cannot prove that Defendant breached a duty of care under the two theories of liability pled in the complaint - direct or vicarious liability. The burden then shifted to Plaintiff. Plaintiff did not establish that there is a triable issue of material fact under either theory pled. Although Plaintiff established that there is a triable issue of fact as to the ownership of the vehicle, Plaintiff has not adequately pled the elements of Veh. Code sec. 17150 in the complaint. Therefore, Plaintiff is granted 45 days leave to amend the complaint, if she can in good faith, to allege the elements of Veh. Code sec. 17150. "If summary judgment is granted on the ground that the complaint is legally insufficient, but it appears from the materials submitted in opposition to the motion that the plaintiff could state a cause of action, the trial court should give the plaintiff an opportunity to amend the complaint before entry of judgment." (Bostrom v. County of San Bernardino (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 1654, 1663.) Here, summary judgment is granted because Plaintiff has not pled that Defendant gave the driver permission to use the vehicle, a requisite element of Veh. Code sec. 17150. However, Plaintiff's opposition indicates that Plaintiff could state a cause of action. (See Opposition, p. 5, lines 11-15.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Off Calendar Jan-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAROLINE MILLER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Granted. Defendant Miller produces prima facie evidence that she did not own or control the taxi cab that was involved in the subject accident. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 3.) Miller also produces prima facie evidence that she was not in a joint enterprise or partnership with Defendant Sanchez at the time of the accident as to render her vicariously liable for Defendant Sanchez's acts. (UMF 4-8.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiff. Plaintiff's evidence fails to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant is directly or vicariously liability. Evidence that Miller owned a medallion, and a share of corporate Defendant Yellow Cab Cooperative, Inc. at the time of the accident is not dispositive that Defendant was in a joint venture or partnership with Defendant Sanchez. Plaintiff's arguments regarding alter ego and Vehicle Code 17150 will not be considered as the issues are not in Plaintiff's Complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
